data.title: Veeco CEO Makes Significant Stock Sale!
data.description:
New insider activity at Veeco ( (VECO) ) has taken place on December 5, 2025.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

CEO William John Miller has recently sold 25,000 shares of Veeco stock, amounting to a total transaction value of $800,000.

Recent Updates on VECO stock

Veeco Instruments has recently experienced significant developments that have influenced its stock performance. The company announced that a leading semiconductor memory company selected its laser spike annealing technology for evaluation, marking a step towards high-volume manufacturing in the DRAM market. This news, alongside a robust Q3 earnings report where Veeco exceeded revenue and earnings expectations, has positively impacted market sentiment. The company’s revenue was $166 million, surpassing expectations, and its non-GAAP EPS was $0.36, above the guidance midpoint of $0.28. Additionally, Veeco’s strategic merger with Axcelis Technologies is anticipated to create a leading semiconductor equipment company with a combined market of over $5 billion. Despite these positive developments, challenges remain, such as a decline in semiconductor and compound semiconductor revenues and an expected decrease in gross margins due to product mix changes. Citi raised Veeco’s price target, reflecting increased wafer fab equipment estimates driven by rising AI investments, indicating a constructive outlook on the sector.

Spark’s Take on VECO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VECO is a Neutral.

Veeco’s overall stock score is driven by a strong earnings call performance and positive technical indicators. However, financial performance concerns and a high P/E ratio weigh down the score. The pending merger with Axcelis Technologies presents potential growth opportunities, but challenges in certain market segments and cash flow management remain.

data.title: IDT Corporation Reports Record Earnings and Positive Outlook
data.description:
IDT Corporation ((IDT)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

IDT Corporation’s recent earnings call conveyed a positive sentiment, highlighting a strong financial performance marked by record gross profit and adjusted EBITDA. The company experienced significant growth in its key segments, despite facing some challenges such as a decline in certain revenue streams and a reduction in cash holdings. Overall, the achievements outweighed the obstacles, maintaining a positive outlook.

Record Levels of Gross Profit and Adjusted EBITDA

IDT Corporation reported impressive financial results in the first quarter, achieving record levels of gross profit and adjusted EBITDA. The adjusted EBITDA reached $37.9 million, marking a 26% increase year-over-year, which underscores the company’s robust financial health and operational efficiency.

Strong Performance in Growth Segments

The growth segments, including NRS, Fintech, and net2phone, collectively grew by 16%, significantly contributing to the consolidated revenue. The strong performance of these segments, particularly NRS and Fintech, played a crucial role in driving the company’s overall revenue growth.

NRS Recurring Revenue Growth

NRS demonstrated substantial growth in recurring revenue, which increased by 22% year-over-year. This growth was driven by a 38% increase in merchant services and a 30% rise in SaaS fees, highlighting the segment’s expanding market presence and customer base.

Fintech Segment Revenue and Profit Increase

The Fintech segment showed remarkable improvement, with income from operations nearly doubling year-over-year, increasing by 97% to $6 million. Additionally, the adjusted EBITDA for this segment climbed 87% to $7.5 million, reflecting its growing profitability and strategic importance.

Resolution of Straight Path Litigation

A significant positive development for IDT was the resolution of the Straight Path litigation. The Delaware Supreme Court affirmed the dismissal of all claims against IDT in the class action suit, providing a favorable outcome and removing a legal overhang.

Decline in NRS Advertising and Data Revenue

Despite the overall positive performance, IDT faced a 15% decline in advertising and data revenue within the NRS segment. This decline poses a challenge that the company may need to address to sustain its growth momentum.

Reduction in Cash Holdings

IDT experienced a reduction in its cash, cash equivalents, debt securities, and current equity investments by $34 million compared to the previous quarter. This decrease in cash holdings may impact the company’s financial flexibility in the short term.

Forward-Looking Guidance

IDT Corporation provided optimistic forward-looking guidance, expecting to achieve a full-year adjusted EBITDA of $141 million to $145 million, indicating a 7% to 10% growth year-over-year. The company ended the quarter with $220 million in cash and investments, and its growth segments are expected to continue contributing significantly to revenue and profitability.

In summary, IDT Corporation’s earnings call reflected a positive sentiment, driven by record financial performance and strong growth in key segments. Despite some challenges, such as declining revenue in certain areas and reduced cash holdings, the company’s achievements and forward-looking guidance suggest a promising outlook for the future.

data.title: Smith & Wesson Earnings Call: Mixed Results and Future Outlook
data.description:
Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. ((SWBI)) has held its Q2 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Smith & Wesson Brands, Inc. recently held its earnings call, revealing a mixed sentiment. The company celebrated strong performance in handgun sales, effective inventory management, and increased average selling prices. However, these positives were tempered by challenges such as a decline in net sales, gross margin, and net income, alongside rising tariff costs.

Strong Handgun Performance

Handgun sales were a bright spot for Smith & Wesson, outperforming market expectations with a 7.7% increase in retail counter sell-through and a 2.1% rise in average selling prices. This segment’s success highlights the company’s strategic positioning in the firearms market.

Inventory Management Success

The company reported a significant reduction in inventory levels, decreasing from $196 million to $183 million. This achievement was accompanied by the generation of over $27 million in operating cash flow, underscoring effective inventory management practices.

New Product Success

New products played a crucial role in the company’s performance, accounting for nearly 40% of sales in the quarter. This demonstrates the effectiveness of Smith & Wesson’s innovation strategy in driving revenue.

Improved Average Selling Prices

The average selling prices (ASPs) saw an overall increase of 3.5% year-over-year. Notably, long guns experienced a 10.2% rise in ASPs, while handguns saw a 2.1% increase, reflecting the company’s pricing power.

Decline in Net Sales

Despite some positive trends, net sales for the second quarter fell to $124.7 million, marking a 3.9% decrease compared to the previous year. This decline poses a challenge for the company moving forward.

Gross Margin Decrease

The company’s gross margin decreased by 2.3% from the previous year, primarily due to decreased absorption on temporarily lower production levels. This highlights a potential area for improvement in operational efficiency.

Increased Tariff Costs

Smith & Wesson faced an 80 basis point negative impact from tariffs, with expectations for this impact to grow in the second half of the fiscal year. This presents a financial headwind that the company will need to navigate.

Net Income Decline

Net income saw a significant decline, dropping to $1.9 million from $4.5 million in the same period last year. This resulted in a decrease in earnings per share from 10¢ to 4¢, reflecting the financial pressures faced by the company.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Smith & Wesson anticipates sales growth of 8% to 10% in the upcoming third quarter compared to the previous year’s Q3. The company expects gross margins to improve, driven by its focus on innovation, strong brand presence, and disciplined inventory management. These forecasts suggest a cautiously optimistic outlook for the future.

In summary, Smith & Wesson’s earnings call painted a picture of both opportunities and challenges. While strong handgun sales and successful inventory management are promising, the decline in net sales and increased tariff costs pose significant hurdles. The company’s forward-looking guidance offers hope for improvement, emphasizing growth and innovation as key drivers.
